body:
## Problem

A maintainer can leave a valid follow-up patch request on a PR after it has already been merged, then invoke `@sepo-agent /orchestrate`. Today the orchestrator accepts the route but stops with `Handoff stop: pull request is merged`, so no new work starts and the user only sees reactions/logs.

Recent example: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/39#issuecomment-4348146105

## Desired behavior

`/orchestrate` should be able to turn a post-merge/closed-PR follow-up into new branch-change work against the default branch, rather than stopping. A practical implementation could either:

- create a tracking issue containing the request text and source PR/comment links, then dispatch the existing implement workflow for that issue; or
- dispatch a dedicated follow-up implementation workflow that opens a fresh PR directly.

## Acceptance criteria

- `@sepo-agent /orchestrate` on a merged/closed PR with a patch/fix request starts new implementation work against `main`.
- The source PR gets an explanatory comment linking the new issue/run/PR.
- Ambiguous non-code requests still stop safely with a visible explanation.
- Add regression coverage for the merged/closed PR orchestration path.

comments.body:
Let's work on it -- also see #33 and its latest comment

@sepo-agent /orchestrate

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `implement` (round 2/10).

manual orchestrate start on issue; dispatching implement

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777506638713 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxMzk4NDIxOTU6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6NDM6aW1wbGVtZW50OjQzOjI -->

**Sepo implementation finished**

- Branch: `agent/implement-issue-43/codex-25139861026`
- Pull request: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/45

Implemented closed/merged PR orchestration follow-ups: `/orchestrate` now creates a linked issue and dispatches implementation for concrete patch requests, posts visible stop comments for ambiguous closed-PR requests, forwards source comment URLs, and keeps delegated route authorization before side effects. Verified with `npm --prefix .agent run build`, `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`, and `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js`.

title: Allow /orchestrate follow-up patches from merged or closed PRs
